en: Submits an HTTP request to the given URL.
* `[%string%]` is the HTTP method to use to send the request. If omitted, defaults to "get".
* Headers must be in the format "Header-Name: Value".
Returns a httpresponse object that contains information from the last completed [http request](https://skripthub.net/docs/?id=2807).
Returns the status codes of one or more http responses.
Returns the status messages of one or more http responses.
Returns the headers of one or more http responses in the format **`"Header-Name: Value"`**.
Multiple http responses may be passed to this expression, but the resulting headers will be combined into a single list with no way to associate each header with its original httpresponse.
Returns the header keys (names) of one or more http responses. This expression is best used in conjunction with [Response Header Values](https://skripthub.net/docs/?id=2814).
Multiple http responses may be passed to this expression, but the resulting keys will be combined into a single list with no way to associate each key with its original http response.
Returns the header values of one or more http responses. This expression is best used in conjunction with [Response Header Keys](https://skripthub.net/docs/?id=2813).
Multiple http responses may be passed to this expression, but the resulting values will be combined into a single list with no way to associate each value with its original http response.
Returns the values of a specific header for one or more http responses.
Multiple http responses may be passed to this expression, but the resulting values will be combined into a single list with no way to associate each value with its original http response.
Returns the bodies of one or more http responses.
Converts a text into a text safe for usage in URLs. This can be useful for injecting user input into a URL (e.g. when using a search api).
Converts a text into a text safe for usage in JSON strings. This can be useful for injecting user input into a JSON payload (e.g. when using a search api).
